methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $136M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Aalo Atomics, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Matt Loszak, is evaluated against a comp universe of 3 public and 8 private peers in the nuclear sector, including Type One Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $136M to date.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach, using the $136M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B to subsequent-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $258M. The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables; while 8 same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide medians unreliably broad.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $220M (low, representing 85% haircut on the stage-progression output) to $297M (high, representing 15% premium), with a median point of $258M. Key uncertainties include the validity of total raised as a valuation proxy absent disclosed prior round prices, reliance on a single methodological pillar, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the nuclear energy tech sector, which limits comparable trading and financing data.
